title: Water Chiller
description: Water-cooled or air-cooled chiller for central plant chilled water production. Includes compressor(s), evaporator, condenser, expansion devices, controls, and safety systems.

questions.question_text: Chiller is level and properly isolated on vibration isolators or inertia base
Clearances for service and tube pull verified per manufacturer
Nameplate data recorded and matches design
Shipping brackets and transit bolts removed
All panels, access doors, and guards installed
Chilled water piping connections tight, no leaks observed
Condenser water piping connections tight, no leaks observed
CHW and CDW flow switches installed and wired
Isolation and balance valves installed and accessible
Pressure gauges installed on evaporator and condenser
Refrigerant charge verified per manufacturer (nameplate or log)
Refrigerant piping insulated, no damage or leaks
Oil level verified in compressor sight glass
Relief valves installed and piped per code
Voltage verified at chiller disconnect (within ±10%)
Voltage balance across phases verified (<2% imbalance)
All electrical connections tight, no exposed conductors
Control power fused and operational
Starter or VFD operational and programmed per sequence
Emergency stop (if provided) tested and functional
Chiller controller powers up and displays correctly
Temperature sensors calibrated and reading correctly
Flow switches tested and interlocked (chiller lockout on loss of flow)
High/low pressure cutouts tested and operational
Low refrigerant temperature cutout tested
Oil pressure safety (if applicable) tested
BAS communication verified (points list reviewed)
Chilled water system chemically cleaned and flushed
Condenser water treatment system installed and operational
Water quality tested and within manufacturer limits (pH, conductivity, hardness)
Factory startup completed by authorized representative
Chiller runs without abnormal noise or vibration
Operating pressures and temperatures within normal range
Capacity control (staging or modulation) verified
Compressor oil level and pressure normal during operation
All alarms and safeties reset and logged in controller
Startup report provided by manufacturer rep

functionalTests.name: Manual Start/Stop Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify chiller can be started and stopped manually from local control panel.

rows.step: Verify CHW and CDW pumps running and flow established
rows.expected: Both pumps running, flow switches proven

rows.step: Initiate manual start from chiller control panel
Observe startup sequence and time delays
rows.expected: Startup per manufacturer sequence, no faults

rows.step: Verify chiller reaches stable operation
rows.expected: Operating pressures and temps stabilize within normal range

rows.step: Initiate manual stop
rows.expected: Chiller stops, compressor de-energizes, no alarms

functionalTests.name: Automatic Operation and Temperature Control
functionalTests.description: Verify chiller starts/stops on BAS command and maintains chilled water temperature setpoint.

rows.step: Set CHW supply temperature setpoint via BAS (e.g., 44°F)
Enable chiller via BAS
Monitor CHW supply temperature as load varies
rows.expected: Chiller modulates capacity to maintain setpoint ±2°F

rows.step: Verify BAS receives real-time feedback (status, temps, alarms)
Disable chiller via BAS
rows.expected: Chiller stops, status reported to BAS

functionalTests.name: Condenser Water Control
functionalTests.description: Verify chiller modulates condenser water valve or fan speed (if air-cooled) to maintain condenser pressure/temperature.

rows.step: Start chiller under normal load
Observe condenser water temperature or head pressure
rows.expected: Chiller modulates CDW valve or fan to maintain setpoint

rows.step: Simulate low ambient (if applicable) or reduce CDW flow
rows.expected: Chiller adjusts to prevent low head pressure

rows.step: Verify no low-pressure alarms during modulation

functionalTests.name: Capacity Control and Staging
functionalTests.description: Verify chiller modulates capacity (VFD, unloading, or staging) in response to load.

rows.step: Start chiller at low load (CHW temp above setpoint)
Increase load (raise CHW return temp or increase flow)
rows.expected: Chiller increases capacity (VFD ramps up or stages on)

rows.step: Decrease load
rows.expected: Chiller decreases capacity (VFD ramps down or stages off)

rows.step: Verify smooth transitions, no cycling or hunting

functionalTests.name: Low Load / Minimum Run Time
functionalTests.description: Verify chiller operates stably at low load and respects minimum run timers.

rows.step: Start chiller with minimal cooling load
rows.expected: Chiller starts and runs at minimum capacity

rows.step: Attempt to stop chiller before minimum run timer expires
rows.expected: Chiller continues to run until timer satisfied

rows.step: Verify chiller does not short-cycle

functionalTests.name: High Pressure Cutout Test
functionalTests.description: Verify high refrigerant pressure safety shuts down chiller and alarms.

rows.step: Start chiller under normal operation
Simulate high head pressure (restrict condenser water flow or disable cooling)
Verify chiller trips on high pressure cutout
rows.expected: Chiller stops, high pressure alarm generated

rows.step: Verify alarm reported to BAS and logged locally
rows.expected: Alarm visible in BAS and chiller log

rows.step: Reset condition and clear alarm
rows.expected: Chiller can be restarted after reset

functionalTests.name: Low Pressure Cutout Test
functionalTests.description: Verify low refrigerant pressure safety shuts down chiller and alarms.

rows.step: Start chiller under normal operation
Simulate low suction pressure (restrict CHW flow or lower setpoint excessively)
Verify chiller trips on low pressure cutout
rows.expected: Chiller stops, low pressure alarm generated

rows.step: Verify alarm reported to BAS and logged locally
rows.expected: Alarm visible in BAS and chiller log

rows.step: Reset condition and clear alarm
rows.expected: Chiller can be restarted after reset

functionalTests.name: Flow Switch Interlock Test
functionalTests.description: Verify loss of CHW or CDW flow prevents chiller start or shuts down running chiller.

rows.step: With chiller off, close CHW isolation valve to simulate no flow
Attempt to start chiller
rows.expected: Chiller fails to start, flow alarm generated

rows.step: Restore CHW flow and start chiller
While running, close CDW isolation valve
Verify chiller shuts down on loss of condenser water flow
rows.expected: Chiller stops, CDW flow alarm generated

rows.step: Restore flow and reset

functionalTests.name: BAS Points Verification
functionalTests.description: Verify all chiller monitoring and control points are communicating accurately with the BAS.

title: Cooling Tower Template
description: Open-circuit cooling tower for condenser water heat rejection. Commission per ASHRAE Guideline 0 and 202.

questions.question_text: Tower installed level and properly supported per drawings
Basin clean and free of debris
Fill media installed correctly without damage
Drift eliminators installed and secure
Fan rotation verified correct
Fan guard in place and secure
Make-up water line connected and functional
Overflow drain installed and piped to waste
Blowdown valve installed and accessible
Condenser water supply piping connected and supported
Condenser water return piping connected
Isolation valves installed and accessible
Piping flushed and clean
System filled and vented
Water treatment system installed and operational
Power wiring to fan motor correct voltage and phase
VFD installed, powered, and programmed
Basin heater wired and functional
Leaving water temp sensor installed and wired to BAS
Flow switch installed and wired
BAS control points verified (start/stop, speed, status)
Low water level cutout functional
High water level alarm functional
Freeze protection alarm set and functional
VFD alarms integrated to BAS

functionalTests.name: Normal Cooling Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify cooling tower operates to maintain condenser water leaving temperature setpoint under normal load.

rows.step: Enable cooling tower via BAS
rows.expected: Tower enabled, status confirmed at BAS

rows.step: Verify condenser water flow established
Start cooling tower fan
rows.expected: Fan starts, runs smoothly without vibration

rows.step: Monitor leaving water temperature as load increases
Verify VFD modulates fan speed to maintain setpoint
rows.expected: Fan speed increases with load to maintain temp

rows.step: Verify BAS trend logs record temp, fan speed, status

functionalTests.name: Fan Speed Modulation
functionalTests.description: Verify VFD modulates fan speed in response to leaving water temperature.

rows.step: Set leaving water temp setpoint to design value
Simulate increased load (or allow actual load)
rows.expected: Leaving water temp begins to rise

rows.step: Observe fan speed increase via VFD
rows.expected: Fan speed ramps up smoothly (no hunting)

rows.step: Verify leaving water temp stabilizes at setpoint
Reduce load

functionalTests.name: Low Water Level Cutout
functionalTests.description: Verify tower shuts down on low water level to protect equipment.

rows.step: Tower operating in normal mode
Simulate low water level in basin (or lower actual level)
Verify tower fan shuts down immediately
Verify alarm sent to BAS
rows.expected: Low water alarm displayed at BAS

rows.step: Restore water level to normal
rows.expected: Alarm clears, tower ready to restart

rows.step: Restart tower via BAS

functionalTests.name: Freeze Protection (Basin Heater)
functionalTests.description: Verify basin heater energizes when outdoor temperature drops to prevent freezing.

rows.step: Tower off, outdoor temp above freeze protection setpoint
Lower outdoor air temp below freeze protection setpoint (simulate or actual)
Verify basin water temperature maintained above freezing
Verify BAS logs heater status

functionalTests.name: Water Treatment System Integration
functionalTests.description: Verify automatic water treatment (chemical feed, blowdown) operates as designed.

rows.step: Tower in normal operation
Verify automatic blowdown cycles per timer or conductivity
rows.expected: Blowdown valve opens on schedule or high conductivity

rows.step: Verify make-up water valve opens to replace blowdown
Verify chemical feed system dosing (if automated)
Test water chemistry (pH, conductivity, inhibitor level)
rows.expected: Parameters within acceptable range per spec

title: Hot Water Boiler Template
description: Gas-fired hot water boiler for heating water system. Commission per ASHRAE Guideline 0 and 202.

questions.question_text: Boiler installed level and properly supported per drawings
Clearances to combustibles meet code and manufacturer requirements
Boiler room ventilation adequate per code
Pressure relief valve installed and piped to drain
Expansion tank installed and pre-charged
System filled, vented, and pressurized
Flue/vent pipe installed per code and manufacturer requirements
Condensate drain (if condensing boiler) piped correctly
Gas piping sized per code and design
Gas piping pressure tested and leak-free
Manual gas shutoff valve installed and accessible
Gas regulator set to proper inlet pressure
Drip leg installed on gas line
Gas valve operation verified (open/close)
Supply and return piping connected correctly
Isolation valves installed on supply and return
Piping flushed and clean
System water treatment installed and operational
Air vents installed at high points
Circulating pump rotation correct
Flow balanced per design
Power supply voltage correct and phase verified
Control wiring per manufacturer wiring diagram
Supply and return water temp sensors installed and wired to BAS
System pressure sensor wired to BAS
Flow switch installed and wired
BAS control points verified (enable, status, alarms)
Boiler controller programmed per design sequence
Low water cutoff functional and wired to burner lockout
High limit temperature switch functional
Pressure relief valve operational
Flame safeguard system functional
Flow proving switch functional (no flow = no fire)
Gas pressure switch functional
Vent damper or vent safety switch (if equipped) functional
Manufacturer startup completed and documented
Combustion analysis performed
CO₂ percentage within manufacturer spec
O₂ percentage within manufacturer spec
Flue gas temperature recorded
Burner firing rate verified at high fire
Burner firing rate verified at low fire (if modulating)
Efficiency calculated and meets or exceeds design

functionalTests.name: Normal Heating Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify boiler fires and maintains supply water temperature setpoint under normal load.

rows.step: Enable boiler via BAS
rows.expected: Boiler enabled, status confirmed at BAS

rows.step: Verify system flow via flow switch
rows.expected: Flow switch proves flow, burner enabled

rows.step: Initiate call for heat (lower SWT setpoint or allow actual load)
Monitor supply water temperature rise
Verify burner modulates (if modulating) to maintain setpoint
rows.expected: Burner modulates between low and high fire as needed

rows.step: Verify supply water temp maintained at setpoint ±3°F
Verify BAS trend logs record temps, firing rate, status

functionalTests.name: Burner Modulation (if modulating)
functionalTests.description: Verify burner modulates firing rate in response to load.

rows.step: Boiler in normal operation at part load
Increase heating load (lower setpoint or open more zones)
Verify burner firing rate increases to high fire
rows.expected: Burner ramps to high fire smoothly

rows.step: Verify SWT returns to setpoint
Decrease heating load
rows.expected: Burner modulates down or cycles off

rows.step: Verify no short-cycling or hunting
rows.expected: Burner modulates smoothly without rapid on/off

functionalTests.name: Low Water Cutoff Test
functionalTests.description: Verify boiler shuts down on low water condition to prevent damage.

rows.step: Boiler operating in normal heating mode
Simulate low water condition (lower pressure or trip LWCO manually)
Verify burner shuts down immediately
Verify alarm sent to BAS
Restore water level and reset LWCO
Restart boiler via BAS

functionalTests.name: High Limit Safety Test
functionalTests.description: Verify boiler shuts down on high temperature limit to prevent overheating.

rows.step: Boiler firing in normal mode
Simulate high limit condition (raise setpoint above limit or disable load)
rows.expected: Supply water temp reaches high limit setpoint

rows.step: Verify burner shuts down on high limit
rows.expected: Burner off, high limit switch opens

rows.step: Verify alarm sent to BAS
Allow SWT to drop below limit
rows.expected: High limit switch closes, ready to restart

rows.step: Verify boiler resumes firing when temp drops
rows.expected: Burner restarts automatically or via BAS

functionalTests.name: Proof of Flow Interlock
functionalTests.description: Verify burner will not fire without proven water flow.

rows.step: Boiler enabled via BAS, call for heat active
Close isolation valve or stop pump to simulate no-flow
Verify burner does not fire or shuts down if already firing
Verify alarm sent to BAS
Restore flow
Verify boiler resumes normal operation

functionalTests.name: Outdoor Air Reset (if applicable)
functionalTests.description: Verify supply water temperature setpoint resets based on outdoor air temperature.

rows.step: Set boiler to OA reset mode in BAS
At design outdoor temp (e.g., 0°F), verify SWT setpoint at design high (e.g., 180°F)
Increase OAT to mild condition (e.g., 50°F)
rows.expected: SWT setpoint decreases per reset schedule (e.g., 140°F)

rows.step: Verify boiler maintains new lower setpoint
Return OAT to cold condition
rows.expected: SWT setpoint increases back to high limit

title: Chilled Water Pump
description: Chilled water circulation pump for hydronic cooling system distribution

questions.question_text: Pump model, size, and capacity match design documents
Pump rotation is correct
All piping connections are complete and leak-free
Suction and discharge isolation valves installed
Balancing valve installed in discharge
Check valve installed (if applicable)
Flexible connectors installed at suction and discharge
Pump base is level and grouted
Alignment completed and documented
Strainer installed and clean
Motor voltage matches nameplate
Motor starter/VFD installed per specifications
Overload protection set correctly
VFD parameters programmed per sequence of operations
All sensors installed and wired (flow, pressure, temperature)
Start/stop commands functional from BAS
Speed control functional (if VFD)
All alarms and status points communicate to BAS
Hand-Off-Auto switch functional
Low flow cutout installed and functional
Low suction pressure cutout functional (if applicable)
Motor overload protection functional
Freeze protection sensors installed (if applicable)
Pump run status proven before valve opens
Pump primed and vented
System filled and air purged
Pump operates smoothly without vibration or noise
No leaks observed during operation
Motor amperage within nameplate FLA
Discharge pressure within design range
Flow rate within design range

functionalTests.name: Manual Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify pump can be started and stopped manually at the local control panel

rows.step: Place Hand-Off-Auto switch in HAND position
Verify discharge pressure rises
Verify flow is established
rows.expected: Flow sensor reads within design range

rows.step: Verify motor amperage
Place switch in OFF position

functionalTests.name: Automatic Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify pump responds to BAS start/stop commands

rows.step: Place Hand-Off-Auto switch in AUTO position
Command pump to start via BAS
Verify run status at BAS
Command pump to stop via BAS
Verify stopped status at BAS

functionalTests.name: VFD Speed Control
functionalTests.description: Verify VFD responds to speed commands and modulates correctly

rows.step: Start pump in AUTO mode
Command VFD to 50% speed via BAS
rows.expected: VFD ramps to 50%, motor amperage decreases proportionally

rows.step: Verify discharge pressure modulates
Command VFD to 100% speed
rows.expected: VFD ramps to 100%, flow and pressure increase

rows.step: Command VFD to minimum speed

functionalTests.name: Low Flow Cutout
functionalTests.description: Verify pump shuts down on low flow condition

rows.step: Start pump in AUTO mode
rows.expected: Pump runs normally with adequate flow

rows.step: Slowly close discharge valve to simulate low flow
Observe pump response
rows.expected: Pump shuts down within time delay period

rows.step: Verify BAS alarm
rows.expected: Low flow alarm generated at BAS

rows.step: Open discharge valve fully and reset alarm

functionalTests.name: BAS Points Verification
functionalTests.description: Verify all BAS points read accurately

title: Condenser Water Pump
description: Condenser water pump commissioning template for chiller condenser water loop circulation.

questions.question_text: Pump is properly mounted and anchored to base/pad
Alignment is correct between motor and pump
Piping connections are complete with no leaks
Isolation valves are installed and operational
Strainer is installed upstream of pump
Suction and discharge gauges installed and operational
Flow meter installed and operational
Pump casing has been filled/primed
Motor rotation verified correct (clockwise looking at shaft)
Vibration isolators installed correctly
Electrical connections to motor complete and tight
Motor voltage matches nameplate requirements
VFD is installed and wired correctly
VFD parameters programmed per design (min/max speed, ramp)
Control signals wired to BAS (start/stop, speed, status)
Flow sensor wired and calibrated
Differential pressure sensor wired and calibrated
Hand-Off-Auto switch functional
Run status indication functional at BAS
Alarm points programmed (low flow, high vibration, VFD fault)
Motor overload protection set correctly per FLA
Low flow cutout switch installed and wired
Pump run interlock with associated chiller verified
VFD fault shutdown stops pump
Emergency stop button functional (if provided)
System flushed and chemically treated
Strainer cleaned after initial run
Pump operated and no unusual noise or vibration
Amp draw recorded and within nameplate FLA
Flow rate measured and within design range
Differential pressure across pump recorded
No leaks observed during operation
Bearing temperature normal after 1 hour run

functionalTests.name: Manual Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify pump can be started and stopped manually using HOA switch in Hand mode.

rows.step: Place HOA switch in HAND position
rows.expected: Pump starts immediately at fixed speed

rows.step: Observe pump operation for 2 minutes
rows.expected: Pump runs smoothly with no unusual noise or vibration

rows.step: Record motor amps
Record suction and discharge pressures
rows.expected: Pressures stable and within design range

rows.step: Place HOA switch in OFF position

functionalTests.name: Automatic Operation with Chiller
functionalTests.description: Verify pump starts and stops automatically with associated chiller per control sequence.

rows.step: Place HOA switch in AUTO position
Start associated chiller
rows.expected: Condenser water pump starts (lead or lag per sequence)

rows.step: Verify BAS shows pump run status
Allow system to stabilize for 5 minutes
Stop chiller
rows.expected: Condenser water pump stops after time delay

functionalTests.name: VFD Speed Control
functionalTests.description: Verify VFD modulates pump speed in response to control signal.

rows.step: Start pump in AUTO mode
Command VFD to 50% speed via BAS
rows.expected: VFD ramps to 50% speed, flow decreases proportionally

rows.step: Command VFD to 75% speed
Command VFD to 100% speed
Command VFD to minimum speed
rows.expected: VFD ramps down to minimum speed setting

rows.step: Verify speed feedback signal at BAS
rows.expected: BAS speed feedback matches commanded speed ±5%

functionalTests.name: Low Flow Cutout
functionalTests.description: Verify pump shuts down on low flow condition.

rows.step: Start pump in AUTO mode and allow to stabilize
Slowly close discharge isolation valve to simulate low flow
Continue closing valve until flow alarm setpoint reached
rows.expected: Low flow alarm activates at BAS

rows.step: Observe pump response
rows.expected: Pump shuts down after time delay (typically 30-60 sec)

rows.step: Open valve fully and reset alarm
rows.expected: Alarm clears, pump can be restarted

functionalTests.name: BAS Points Verification
functionalTests.description: Verify all condenser water pump BAS points are reading accurately.

title: Hot Water Pump
description: Hot water circulation pump for hydronic heating system distribution

questions.question_text: Pump model, size, and capacity match design documents
Pump rotation is correct
All piping connections are complete and leak-free
Suction and discharge isolation valves installed
Balancing valve installed in discharge
Check valve installed (if applicable)
Flexible connectors installed at suction and discharge
Pump base is level and grouted
Alignment completed and documented
Strainer installed and clean
Motor voltage matches nameplate
Motor starter/VFD installed per specifications
Overload protection set correctly
VFD parameters programmed per sequence of operations
All sensors installed and wired (flow, pressure, temperature)
Start/stop commands functional from BAS
Speed control functional (if VFD)
All alarms and status points communicate to BAS
Hand-Off-Auto switch functional
Low flow cutout installed and functional
High temperature cutout functional
Motor overload protection functional
Freeze protection sensors installed (if applicable)
Pump run status proven before valve opens
Pump primed and vented
System filled and air purged
Pump operates smoothly without vibration or noise
No leaks observed during operation
Motor amperage within nameplate FLA
Discharge pressure within design range
Flow rate within design range

functionalTests.name: Manual Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify pump can be started and stopped manually at the local control panel

rows.step: Place Hand-Off-Auto switch in HAND position
Verify discharge pressure rises
Verify flow is established
rows.expected: Flow sensor reads within design range

rows.step: Verify motor amperage
Place switch in OFF position

functionalTests.name: Automatic Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify pump responds to BAS start/stop commands

rows.step: Place Hand-Off-Auto switch in AUTO position
Command pump to start via BAS
Verify run status at BAS
Command pump to stop via BAS
Verify stopped status at BAS

functionalTests.name: VFD Speed Control
functionalTests.description: Verify VFD responds to speed commands and modulates correctly

rows.step: Start pump in AUTO mode
Command VFD to 50% speed via BAS
rows.expected: VFD ramps to 50%, motor amperage decreases proportionally

rows.step: Verify discharge pressure modulates
Command VFD to 100% speed
rows.expected: VFD ramps to 100%, flow and pressure increase

rows.step: Command VFD to minimum speed

functionalTests.name: Low Flow Cutout
functionalTests.description: Verify pump shuts down on low flow condition

rows.step: Start pump in AUTO mode
rows.expected: Pump runs normally with adequate flow

rows.step: Slowly close discharge valve to simulate low flow
Observe pump response
rows.expected: Pump shuts down within time delay period

rows.step: Verify BAS alarm
rows.expected: Low flow alarm generated at BAS

rows.step: Open discharge valve fully and reset alarm

functionalTests.name: High Temperature Cutout
functionalTests.description: Verify pump shuts down on high temperature condition

rows.step: Start pump in AUTO mode with hot water system operational
Simulate high temperature condition (close isolation valve or adjust boiler setpoint)
Observe pump response
rows.expected: Pump shuts down within time delay period

rows.step: Verify BAS alarm
rows.expected: High temperature alarm generated at BAS

rows.step: Restore normal conditions and reset alarm

functionalTests.name: BAS Points Verification
functionalTests.description: Verify all BAS points read accurately

title: Hot Water Unit Heater Template
description: Hot water unit heater for space heating

questions.question_text: Unit mounted securely per manufacturer specs
Hot water piping connected and tested
Control valve installed in correct orientation
Coil vented and system purged of air
Electrical connections complete and voltage correct
Thermostat/controller installed and wired
Control valve operates full stroke
Valve fails to safe position on power loss
Thermostat modulates valve correctly
Fan cycles with heating demand
Hot water flow verified at design rate
Fan rotation correct, no unusual noise
Unit heats space effectively

functionalTests.name: Heating Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify unit heater operates correctly and maintains setpoint

rows.step: Set thermostat to call for heat
Verify hot water flow through coil
Measure supply air temperature
Monitor valve modulation as space warms
Allow unit to satisfy thermostat

title: Air Handling Unit
description: Air handling unit providing heating, cooling, filtration, and outdoor air ventilation for building zones. Includes supply and return fans, heating and cooling coils, filters, dampers, and full DDC controls integration.

questions.question_text: AHU is properly anchored and leveled per manufacturer instructions
All shipping bracing, blocks, and packaging removed from unit
Unit access doors close and seal properly
Unit interior is clean and free of construction debris
All fan wheels are clean and free of debris
Fan belt tension is correct (if belt-driven)
Fan sheaves properly aligned and set screws tight
Bearings properly lubricated per manufacturer schedule
All coil fins are clean and undamaged
Heating coil piping connections tight and leak-free
Cooling coil piping connections tight and leak-free
Coil drain pans clean and draining properly
Condensate drain lines properly trapped and pitched
Filters installed correctly with proper gasket seal
All dampers operate freely through full stroke
Damper linkages secure and properly adjusted
All vibration isolators properly adjusted
Flexible duct connections installed at supply and return
Supply ductwork installation complete
Return ductwork installation complete
Outside air ductwork installation complete
All duct connections sealed and airtight
Fire/smoke dampers installed per code
Volume dampers installed and accessible for TAB
Duct static pressure sensor installed per design
TAB ports installed at required locations
Power wiring to unit complete and terminated
Motor starters/disconnects labeled and accessible
Supply fan motor rotation verified correct
Return fan motor rotation verified correct
VFDs programmed per manufacturer and design requirements
VFD minimum and maximum speed settings configured
All temperature sensors installed and wired
All pressure sensors installed and wired
Filter differential pressure switch installed and wired
Freeze stat installed in proper location and wired
All damper actuators installed and wired
Heating coil valve and actuator installed and wired
Cooling coil valve and actuator installed and wired
Unit connected to BAS and communicating
All BAS points mapped and trending
Freeze stat set to trip at design temperature
Freeze stat shuts down supply fan and closes outside air damper on alarm
Filter status switch wired to BAS for alarm
Smoke detectors installed per code (if required)
Smoke detector alarm shuts down unit and closes outside air damper
Motor overload protection functional
Disconnect switches lock in OFF position
Manufacturer startup completed and documented
Supply fan amperage recorded and within nameplate rating
Return fan amperage recorded and within nameplate rating
Unit operates without excessive vibration or noise
All sensors reading reasonable values
Heating coil valve modulates smoothly
Cooling coil valve modulates smoothly
All dampers stroke fully and smoothly
Test and Balance (TAB) complete with report submitted
Design airflow achieved at all terminal devices
Minimum outside air CFM verified by TAB

functionalTests.name: Manual Operation Test
functionalTests.description: Verify all components can be manually controlled via BAS and respond correctly.

rows.step: Via BAS, command supply fan ON
rows.expected: Supply fan starts and runs smoothly

rows.step: Via BAS, command return fan ON
rows.expected: Return fan starts and runs smoothly

rows.step: Command supply fan VFD to 50% speed
rows.expected: VFD responds, fan speed reduces, airflow decreases

rows.step: Command supply fan VFD to 100% speed
rows.expected: VFD responds, fan runs at full speed

rows.step: Command outside air damper to 100% open
Command outside air damper to 0% (closed)
Command return air damper to 100% open
Command exhaust air damper to 100% open
Command heating coil valve to 100% open
rows.expected: Valve opens fully, hot water flows

rows.step: Command heating coil valve to 0% (closed)
Command cooling coil valve to 100% open
rows.expected: Valve opens fully, chilled water flows

rows.step: Command cooling coil valve to 0% (closed)
Command unit OFF
rows.expected: All fans stop, all valves close, dampers return to normal positions

functionalTests.name: Occupied Mode - Heating Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify unit responds correctly to heating demand in occupied mode.

rows.step: Set zone thermostat to call for heat (setpoint above space temp)
Verify supply and return fans are enabled
Verify outside air damper modulates to minimum position
rows.expected: OA damper at minimum ventilation position per design

rows.step: Verify return air damper modulates open
rows.expected: RA damper open to maintain economizer sequence

rows.step: Verify cooling coil valve is closed
Verify heating coil valve modulates to maintain supply air setpoint
rows.expected: HW valve modulates, SAT increases toward setpoint

rows.step: Observe supply air temperature
rows.expected: SAT reaches and maintains heating setpoint

rows.step: Verify zone temperature rises
When zone satisfies, verify heating valve closes
rows.expected: HW valve returns to 0% when satisfied

functionalTests.name: Occupied Mode - Cooling Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify unit responds correctly to cooling demand in occupied mode.

rows.step: Set zone thermostat to call for cooling (setpoint below space temp)
Verify supply and return fans are enabled
Verify heating coil valve is closed
If OAT permits, verify economizer dampers modulate for free cooling
rows.expected: OA damper opens, RA damper closes per economizer logic

rows.step: If cooling still needed, verify cooling coil valve modulates to maintain supply air setpoint
rows.expected: CHW valve modulates, SAT decreases toward setpoint

rows.step: Observe supply air temperature
rows.expected: SAT reaches and maintains cooling setpoint

rows.step: Verify zone temperature decreases
When zone satisfies, verify cooling valve closes
rows.expected: CHW valve returns to 0% when satisfied

functionalTests.name: Economizer Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify economizer provides free cooling when outdoor conditions permit.

rows.step: Simulate cooling demand with outside air temperature below economizer high limit
Verify outside air damper modulates open beyond minimum
rows.expected: OA damper opens to provide free cooling

rows.step: Verify return air damper modulates closed
rows.expected: RA damper closes proportionally as OA opens

rows.step: Verify exhaust air damper modulates open
rows.expected: EA damper opens to relieve excess air

rows.step: Verify mixed air temperature is controlled
Verify cooling coil valve remains closed if economizer satisfies load
rows.expected: CHW valve at 0% while economizer is effective

rows.step: Simulate OAT rising above economizer high limit
rows.expected: Economizer disables, dampers return to minimum OA

rows.step: Verify mechanical cooling (CHW valve) takes over
rows.expected: CHW valve modulates to satisfy cooling demand

functionalTests.name: Unoccupied Mode Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify unit operates correctly in unoccupied/night setback mode.

rows.step: Set BAS schedule to unoccupied mode
Verify supply and return fans cycle OFF (unless override active)
rows.expected: Fans shut down in unoccupied mode

rows.step: Verify outside air damper closes
Verify return air damper opens
Verify heating and cooling valves are closed
Simulate space temp drops below unoccupied heating setpoint
rows.expected: Unit cycles ON to provide heating

rows.step: Verify fans start and heating valve modulates
rows.expected: Fans run, HW valve opens to maintain unoccupied heating setpoint

rows.step: When setpoint satisfied, verify unit returns to OFF
rows.expected: Fans stop, valves close after deadband satisfied

rows.step: Simulate space temp rises above unoccupied cooling setpoint
rows.expected: Unit cycles ON to provide cooling

rows.step: Verify fans start and cooling valve modulates
rows.expected: Fans run, CHW valve opens to maintain unoccupied cooling setpoint

functionalTests.name: Supply Duct Static Pressure Control
functionalTests.description: Verify supply fan VFD modulates to maintain duct static pressure setpoint.

rows.step: Enable unit in occupied mode with fans running
Record initial duct static pressure and VFD speed
Simulate increased load by opening VAV boxes or zone dampers
Verify VFD increases fan speed to maintain static pressure setpoint
rows.expected: VFD speed increases, static pressure returns to setpoint

rows.step: Simulate decreased load by closing VAV boxes or zone dampers
Verify VFD decreases fan speed to maintain static pressure setpoint
rows.expected: VFD speed decreases, static pressure returns to setpoint

rows.step: Verify VFD does not drop below minimum speed setting
Verify VFD does not exceed maximum speed setting

functionalTests.name: Freeze Protection
functionalTests.description: Verify freeze stat shuts down unit and protects coils from freezing.

rows.step: Enable unit in occupied mode
Simulate low mixed air temperature by adjusting freeze stat setpoint or sensor input
rows.expected: Mixed air temp drops below freeze stat setpoint

rows.step: Verify freeze stat alarm activates
rows.expected: Freeze stat trips, alarm appears in BAS

rows.step: Verify supply fan shuts down immediately
Verify return fan shuts down immediately
Verify outside air damper closes fully
Verify return air damper opens fully
Verify heating coil valve opens to 100%
rows.expected: HW valve fully open to prevent coil freeze

rows.step: Verify unit remains locked out until manual reset
Reset freeze stat and verify unit can be restarted
rows.expected: After reset, unit returns to normal operation

functionalTests.name: Filter Status Monitoring
functionalTests.description: Verify filter differential pressure switch alarms when filters require replacement.

rows.step: Verify filter differential pressure switch is installed and wired to BAS
With unit running and clean filters installed, verify switch is in normal state
Simulate high filter pressure drop (block filters or adjust switch setpoint)
Verify filter status switch trips
Verify BAS displays filter maintenance alarm
rows.expected: Filter alarm appears on BAS and notifications sent

rows.step: Replace filters or remove blockage
Verify filter alarm clears

functionalTests.name: Smoke/Fire Mode (if applicable)
functionalTests.description: Verify unit responds correctly to smoke detector or fire alarm signal.

rows.step: Enable unit in normal occupied mode
Simulate smoke detector activation (if installed)
rows.expected: Smoke alarm signal sent to unit controller

rows.step: Verify supply fan shuts down
Verify return fan shuts down
Verify outside air damper closes fully
Verify return air damper closes fully (or per smoke control sequence)
rows.expected: RA damper closes per code requirements

rows.step: Verify all coil valves close
Verify unit remains locked out until alarm is reset
rows.expected: Unit will not restart until manual reset

rows.step: Reset smoke alarm and verify unit can resume normal operation
rows.expected: After reset, unit returns to normal control

functionalTests.name: BAS Points Verification
functionalTests.description: Verify all BAS points are mapped correctly, trending, and reporting accurate values.

title: Exhaust Fan
description: Exhaust fan template with full commissioning scope including components, prefunctional checklists, and functional performance tests.

questions.question_text: Fan installed per approved shop drawings and specifications
Fan securely mounted and vibration isolators installed correctly
All shipping brackets and restraints removed
Fan rotation verified correct
Fan belts properly tensioned and aligned (if belt-driven)
Fan wheel secure and balanced
Backdraft damper installed and moves freely
Adequate clearances provided for service
Ductwork connected and sealed
Discharge terminates properly (away from air intakes, operable windows)
Fire/smoke dampers installed and operational (if required)
Balancing dampers installed and accessible
Power wiring complete and voltage verified correct
Motor overload protection set correctly
VFD programmed and parameters set (if applicable)
Control wiring complete and terminated per drawings
Fan status proven contact wired to BAS
Start/stop commands from BAS verified
Interlock with space pressure or occupancy verified (if applicable)
Fan motor amperage measured and within nameplate rating
Airflow measured and meets design
Fan runs smoothly with no abnormal noise or vibration
Backdraft damper closes fully when fan off
VFD operation smooth across speed range (if applicable)

functionalTests.name: Manual Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify fan can be manually started and stopped.

rows.step: Set fan to 'Manual' or 'Hand' mode at controller
Start fan manually
rows.expected: Fan starts; fan status proven at BAS; backdraft damper opens

rows.step: Verify airflow at exhaust grille
Stop fan manually
rows.expected: Fan stops; fan status off at BAS; backdraft damper closes

functionalTests.name: Automatic Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify fan operates per sequence of operations in automatic mode.

rows.step: Set fan to 'Auto' mode
Trigger start condition (occupancy schedule, space pressure, or manual BAS command)
rows.expected: Fan starts automatically; fan status proven

rows.step: Verify interlocks (if applicable, e.g., with MUA or space pressure)
Remove start condition
rows.expected: Fan stops after appropriate time delay (if programmed)

functionalTests.name: VFD Speed Control
functionalTests.description: Verify VFD modulates fan speed per control signal (if VFD-equipped).

rows.step: Start fan in automatic mode with VFD
Command VFD to 50% speed from BAS
rows.expected: VFD ramps to 50%; fan speed reduces; airflow reduces proportionally

rows.step: Command VFD to 100% speed
rows.expected: VFD ramps to 100%; fan speed increases; airflow increases

rows.step: Command VFD to minimum speed
rows.expected: VFD ramps to minimum speed setting

rows.step: Verify smooth operation across speed range with no hunting or instability

functionalTests.name: BAS Points Verification
functionalTests.description: Verify all BAS points are mapped correctly and values are accurate.

title: VAV Terminal Unit with Reheat
description: Variable Air Volume terminal unit with hot water reheat coil for zone temperature control. Includes airflow sensor, damper, actuator, and reheat coil.

questions.question_text: VAV box installed per approved shop drawings and manufacturer instructions
Inlet and discharge duct connections properly sealed
Unit properly supported and vibration isolated
Access for service and testing is adequate
Reheat coil piping installed with isolation valves
Coil vented and drained properly
VAV controller installed and wired per submittal
Airflow sensor installed and calibrated
Zone temperature sensor installed per design
Discharge air temperature sensor installed
Damper actuator wired and responds to control signal
Reheat valve actuator wired and responds to control signal
All points visible in BAS with correct units and scaling
Maximum airflow set and verified per design
Minimum airflow set and verified per design
Damper strokes fully open and closed
Hot water flow verified at design conditions
Zone properly balanced to adjacent zones

functionalTests.name: Cooling Mode Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify VAV box responds correctly to cooling demand by modulating airflow.

rows.step: Set zone thermostat to call for cooling (reduce setpoint below room temp)
rows.expected: VAV damper modulates open to increase airflow

rows.step: Verify airflow increases toward maximum
rows.expected: Measured airflow increases; BAS displays increased CFM

rows.step: Verify reheat valve is fully closed during cooling
rows.expected: Reheat valve position = 0%, no hot water flow

rows.step: Return zone thermostat to setpoint
rows.expected: Damper modulates toward minimum airflow position

functionalTests.name: Heating Mode Operation
functionalTests.description: Verify VAV box responds correctly to heating demand with airflow and reheat sequencing.

rows.step: Set zone thermostat to call for heating (raise setpoint above room temp)
rows.expected: VAV damper modulates toward maximum airflow first

rows.step: If heating demand continues, verify reheat valve begins to open
rows.expected: Reheat valve modulates open; discharge air temp rises

rows.step: Verify zone temperature rises toward setpoint
rows.expected: Zone temp increases; heating demand is satisfied

rows.step: Return zone thermostat to setpoint
rows.expected: Reheat valve closes; damper returns to minimum flow

functionalTests.name: Airflow Tracking and Control
functionalTests.description: Verify airflow sensor and damper control maintain setpoint accurately.

rows.step: Command VAV to minimum airflow setpoint via BAS
rows.expected: Damper modulates to maintain minimum CFM ±10%

rows.step: Measure actual airflow with calibrated instrument
Command VAV to maximum airflow setpoint via BAS
rows.expected: Damper fully open; maximum CFM achieved ±10%

rows.step: Measure actual airflow at maximum
rows.expected: Measured flow matches design and BAS reading ±10%

functionalTests.name: Occupied/Unoccupied Mode
functionalTests.description: Verify VAV responds to occupancy schedule with setback and airflow adjustment.

rows.step: Force system to unoccupied mode via BAS
rows.expected: Zone setpoints adjust to unoccupied heating/cooling setpoints; airflow reduces to minimum

rows.step: Verify VAV damper at minimum position
rows.expected: Damper at minimum; airflow at unoccupied minimum CFM

rows.step: Force system to occupied mode via BAS
rows.expected: Zone setpoints return to occupied; VAV ready to modulate on demand
